C:
Here's a ready-to-paste App Store description. The first three lines are written to survive the "...more" truncation on the store page, so they carry the whole pitch on their own.

---

**Build better habits in the 30 seconds you actually have.**
For busy professionals who don't have time for another complicated app.
Check in once a day, see your streak, move on with your life.

Most habit trackers assume you have hours to spare. You don't. [App Name] is built for packed calendars and back-to-back meetings — log a habit in two taps, get a clear view of what's working, and let the app handle the rest.

**Why professionals choose [App Name]:**
- One-tap check-ins — no menus, no friction, no setup rituals
- Smart reminders that fit your schedule instead of nagging at 9 a.m. sharp
- At-a-glance streaks and weekly trends, so progress is obvious in seconds
- Private by default — your data stays yours
- Apple Watch & widget support to log habits without opening the app

Whether it's a morning workout, daily reading, drinking more water, or shutting the laptop on time, [App Name] keeps you consistent without adding to your mental load.

Small habits, repeated daily, compound into a different life. Start today — it takes less time than reading this.

---

Swap in your real app name for `[App Name]`. If you tell me the app's actual name, the specific habits it targets, or its standout feature (AI coaching, team accountability, etc.), I'll tailor the copy and tighten it to App Store character limits.
